message.content: Adversaries may compromise existing accounts to exploit the trust and relationships associated with those personas. This technique involves taking over accounts on various platforms (e.g., Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitter, Google) rather than creating new ones. Methods for compromising accounts include phishing, purchasing credentials from third-party sites, or brute-forcing passwords, often leveraging information from previous data breaches. Before targeting, adversaries typically conduct reconnaissance to identify valuable accounts. Once compromised, these accounts may be further developed by updating profile information, expanding social networks, or adding photos to enhance credibility. Compromised accounts can be used for various malicious activities, such as phishing campaigns or gathering sensitive information. Mitigation strategies include enforcing strong, unique passwords, implementing multi-factor authentication, monitoring for unusual account activity, and educating users about phishing and social engineering tactics.
